summary: Frontend regression verified for OICE landing iteration 2 (warm editorial redesign, Space Grotesk, scroll-reveal Problem, isometric SVGs, full-bleed orange FinalCTA, new hero copy). All 12 sections render with original data-testids, font-family is Space Grotesk on body + headings, hero copy matches 'Innovation isn't broken.' / 'Coordination is.', DNA graph still renders 7 nodes + 9 edges (Handle fix from iter 1 preserved), 4 solution pillars present, 8 HowItWorks steps render with correct titles and end at opacity 1, WhyNow has 01/02/03 labels + 4 isometric SVGs, Stats counters animate to 5/5/12/90/6/5, Problem scroll-driven word reveal transitions properly (final colors rgb(255,255,255) for white words and rgb(238,105,46) — #ee692e — for the 'black hole.' accent), FinalCTA shows all 3 buttons + lightning ornament, all routing (/, /app, /simulation, back-to-home) and smooth-scroll nav work, mobile menu drawer opens at 390px. 0 console errors and 0 page errors across the full test run.
critical_code_review_comments: Problem.jsx useScroll + useTransform implementation is correct — RevealWord interpolates color via motion.span style which is performant and matches expected behaviour. No errors emitted.
HowItWorks slide-in animation completes to x:0/opacity:1 as designed; verified how-step-8 opacity == 1 after scroll.

FinalCTA outer section uses transparent bg, the warm orange #ee692e is on the inner full-bleed block (confirmed visually). No issue, just noting that any future testing should screenshot the inner block instead of computed style of the outer section element.
All data-testid coverage from iter 1 is preserved; the redesign did not break any selectors. Excellent regression discipline.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 redesign is fully verified — fonts, palette, isometric visuals, scroll-reveal headline, slide-in steps and full-bleed orange CTA all behaved as specified with 0 console errors. DNA graph Handle fix from iter 1 still works. Mobile menu drawer at 390px renders nav links + Launch App correctly (visually confirmed in screenshot; only caveat is that there are duplicate desktop+mobile nav-link-about elements so use .last or scope inside the drawer when asserting visibility). Tested routes: /, /app, /simulation, back-to-home all OK.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration.
